purpose: Top-level renderer for the Chat tab's 'Dashboard' sub-view. Shows agent activity sourced from disk folders Chat/Dashboard/Agent{x}/ via window.electronAPI.getChatDashboardData(). Each agent renders as a vertical 'AgentRow' lane; tasks within a lane are arranged either as a flat horizontal strip (single-wave / fully-parallel agent) or as wave-columns connected by BFS dependency lines (multi-wave agent). Computes within-agent waves using a longest-path topological-levels algorithm where cross-agent depends_on are deliberately ignored (siblings only). Subscribes to the 'chat_dashboard_changed' push channel for live updates and falls back to a six-agent hardcoded demo dataset (loadDemoData) when no agent folders exist or the IPC bridge is unavailable. Handles selection of a task card to open AgentDetails modal, with a flat taskById map built across all lanes so dep-label lookups resolve cross-agent.
gotchas: computeWavesForAgent INTENTIONALLY filters depends_on to within-agent ids — passing a task whose deps reference other agents will silently drop those edges and place the task in wave 0. This is by design (sibling-only BFS) but will look wrong if you mistakenly expect cross-agent ordering.
Demo data is loaded ANY time the IPC call returns 0 agents, not just when the API is missing. A real-but-empty Chat/Dashboard/ directory will display the demo lanes — easy to mistake for live data.
AgentDetails is invoked with progressData=null and projectRoot=null; do not rely on those props inside AgentDetails when launched from the chat dashboard.

The dependency-line SVG is only rendered for hasMultipleWaves; redraw is wired through a ResizeObserver on the tasks container, but NOT to wave/task content mutations beyond the tasks array reference — adding/removing cards inside a wave without changing tasks identity will not redraw lines.
Lanes are sorted alphabetically by name AFTER shaping — agent ordering is not preserved from the IPC payload.
patterns: Status aggregation: lane.status is derived from task statuses (allCompleted -> completed, anyFailed -> failed, anyInProgress -> in_progress, else pending) inside shapeAgent — single source of truth for the row badge.
BFS dependency-line reuse: chat dashboard uses the SAME drawDependencyLines/setupCardHoverEffects helpers as the code dashboard, just scoped to a single lane's container/svg.
Live-updates pattern: `window.electronAPI.on('chat_dashboard_changed', () => loadData())`; the cleanup function returned by the IPC bridge MUST be invoked in useEffect cleanup.
Graceful degradation: demo data is the fallback for both 'no API' and 'API failed' branches — chat dashboard never blanks out.
conventions: Helper components defined below the default export inside the same file (AgentRow at the bottom, demo loader as async function).
Tasks transformed via taskToAgent() before passing to AgentCard — keeps AgentCard contract uniform between code and chat dashboards.
CSS namespace 'chat-' prefix for all chat-dashboard-specific classes (chat-agent-row, chat-wave-column, chat-dashboard-empty, etc.) so they can't collide with the code dashboard's selectors.

related: src/ui/components/chat/ChatSidebar.jsx (sets chatActiveView='dashboard' to mount this view)
src/ui/components/WavePipeline.jsx (analogous wave layout for code dashboards)
